assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)

The report structure is read-only verification: scope containment checks, diff analysis, acceptance criteria validation, and test classification. No file modifications or code changes are described or performed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)

The report states 'All checks pass. The PR correctly implements the PURL recommendation simplification as specified in TC-9105.' — this is a finding statement, not a merge action. No merge commands, approval actions, or auto-merge directives appear anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains the Test Change Classification in the main verification table (line 14) and detailed findings section (lines 85-111), but there is no mention of a 'Style/Conventions sub-agent' or 'Style/Conventions domain' anywhere in any of the output files. The classification is presented as a standalone section in the report without attribution to any specific sub-agent or domain.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 89: 'Structural scan (base-branch vs PR-branch comparison)'. The analysis is structured around file-level content comparison: test function additions/removals (+1/-1), assertion counts (+5/-5), and assertion specificity changes. Line 97: 'Semantic assessment: The removed test test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers tested qualifier-specific behavior...' — this references test behavior, not acceptance criteria. The reductive findings (lines 99-101) reference specific test file content changes. The analysis does not reference acceptance criteria or task requirements for its classification verdict.
